after.evidence_quote: Bronn | Kills | Ser Vardis Egen | Defeats and kills him in trial by combat
after.rationale: Ser Vardis is the knight who was defeated and killed by Bronn in the trial by combat.

after.evidence_quote: Dany is seated beside Drogo on an earthen ramp above the Dothraki.
after.rationale: Drogo is the groom and the Khal presiding over the wedding, seated beside Dany as the ceremony commences.

after.evidence_quote: Theon and the Greatjon drag him before her; he has a gash across his scalp.
after.rationale: The Greatjon is explicitly named alongside Theon as physically dragging Jaime before Catelyn.

after.evidence_quote: A Lysene-looking wine merchant offers Dany a cask of Arbor red... the man hurls the cask at Dany.
after.rationale: The wine merchant physically offered the poisoned cask and then hurled it at Daenerys when pressed to taste it.

after.evidence_quote: Drogo killed Khal Ogo slew him in battle — his khalasar carried out the raid resulting in the burning town and enslaved Lhazareen.
after.rationale: Drogo led the khalasar that conducted the raid on the Lhazareen town; he commanded the forces responsible for the sacking and taking of captives.

after.evidence_quote: He argues it is dishonorable to murder a fourteen-year-old girl and her unborn child, invoking the memory of why they rebelled against Aerys.
after.rationale: Ned is the one who actively opposes the assassination, argues against it on moral grounds, and ultimately resigns his office over it.

after.evidence_quote: Jon vaults onto a table with a dagger and slashes at Thorne's face. / Eastwatch men pull him off; Thorne says 'The boy is a wildling'
after.rationale: Thorne is the direct recipient of Jon's attack — slashed at and grabbed by the throat.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Rodrik, Bronn, Chiggen, and Ser Willis ride to meet them. Ser Rodrik fights the leader in the shadowskin cloak.
after.rationale: Ser Rodrik rides out to meet the clansmen and personally fights their leader in single combat.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Rodrik, Bronn, Chiggen, and Ser Willis ride to meet them.
after.rationale: Ser Willis rides out with Ser Rodrik to engage the attacking clansmen.

after.evidence_quote: Greatjon Umber fired the siege towers
after.rationale: Greatjon Umber personally fired the siege towers.

after.evidence_quote: The Tickler asks the same questions every day... No one survives the questioning.
after.rationale: The Tickler physically conducts the daily interrogations from which no prisoner survives.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Gregor commands The Tickler... They are his men; he selects prisoners, gives orders. The Tickler serves as interrogator for Ser Gregor — Asks the questions; Gregor watches until victim dies.
after.rationale: Ser Gregor commands The Tickler, selects prisoners, and gives orders but does not personally conduct the daily interrogations.

after.evidence_quote: Raff the Sweetling kills the boy's screaming mother.
after.rationale: Raff the Sweetling personally kills the mother after she starts screaming at her son's murder.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Amory dismisses Hoat's promises and orders prisoners sent to the dungeon under the Widow's Tower.
after.rationale: Ser Amory is the commanding recipient who accepts the prisoners and issues orders to send them to the dungeon.

after.evidence_quote: captured Glover and Ser Aenys Frey
after.rationale: Ser Aenys Frey is the second named prisoner captured and delivered to Ser Amory's custody.

after.evidence_quote: Stripped naked, he pleads and sobs; Rorge pulls him loose from captors; Shagwell kicks him into the bear pit.
after.rationale: Ser Amory is stripped naked, dragged loose, and thrown into the bear pit to be killed.

after.evidence_quote: she requests and is granted the honor of arming Renly
after.rationale: Brienne is the one who makes the request and will physically perform the act of arming Renly.

after.evidence_quote: sails lowered, oars deployed per Ser Imry's orders; Ser Imry commands the fleet; Davos follows orders despite misgivings.
after.rationale: Ser Imry commands the fleet and issues the orders for the formation maneuver that Davos and other captains execute.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Meryn Trant kills the defiant knight — Drives longsword through his chest.
after.rationale: Ser Meryn Trant physically executed the killing by driving his longsword through the knight's chest.

after.evidence_quote: Insists that spitting on Theon is spitting on the Drowned God; Benfred must be given to the god 'the old way'
after.rationale: Aeron explicitly insists that Benfred must be given to the Drowned God 'the old way,' framing it as a religious obligation — he is the instigator/orderer, not the executor.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Garlan — Brother of / acts for — Margaery Tyrell — Proposes her marriage on her behalf.
after.rationale: Ser Garlan actively proposes the marriage on Margaery's behalf, making him a co-executor of the betrothal agreement.

after.evidence_quote: More men arrived and have fallen on Ser Rodrik's forces. / Ramsay killed Ser Rodrik: 'When the old fool gave me his hand, I took half his arm instead.'
after.rationale: Ser Rodrik commands the besieging host that is surprised and destroyed; he is personally killed by Ramsay during the engagement.

after.evidence_quote: He asks Theon to take him instead of Beth
after.rationale: Ser Rodrik physically performs the act of offering himself as a substitute hostage.

after.evidence_quote: Maester Luwin is speared between the shoulders by a mounted knight and ridden over.
after.rationale: Maester Luwin is explicitly speared from behind by a mounted Bolton knight during the sack.

after.evidence_quote: Cersei asks about the conspiracy of men plotting to open the gates for Stannis.
after.rationale: The Antler Men are the conspirators actively plotting to open the city gates for Stannis.

after.evidence_quote: He tries to kill me, no mistake; Ser Mandon would have taken off the top of his head if he hadn't pulled back
after.rationale: Ser Mandon Moore physically attempted to kill Tyrion during the battle, nearly taking off the top of his head.

after.evidence_quote: 'Ser Gregor. Dunsen, Polliver, Raff the Sweetling. The Tickler and . . . the Tickler . . . the Hound . . .'
after.rationale: Explicitly named in the recitation as a target of Arya's nightly death-prayer.

after.evidence_quote: 'The Tickler and . . . the Tickler . . . the Hound . . .'
after.rationale: Explicitly named (twice, with trailing hesitation) in the recitation as a target of Arya's nightly death-prayer.

after.evidence_quote: Lem says Thoros cut Beric down before he died.
after.rationale: Lem initiates and performs the dispute, making the direct claim about how Beric died.

after.evidence_quote: Lem says Thoros cut Beric down before he died.
after.rationale: Lem claims Thoros cut Beric down, implicating him as the one who acted on Beric, but this is Lem's disputed assertion rather than confirmed fact within this event.

after.evidence_quote: Lord Beric commands Brotherhood Without Banners — Orders attacks, trials, sentries; everyone obeys; commands respect for hosts' rules.
after.rationale: Lord Beric commands all Brotherhood operations including attacks and sentry disposals; this coordinated pre-dawn assault on the septry (killing rooftop sentinel, door guards, then signaling full attack) is his ordered operation, though no line directly quotes him ordering this specific sub-action.

after.evidence_quote: Thoros shouts, and the outlaws attack in earnest.
after.rationale: Thoros shouts the signal that triggers the full assault, making him the instigator who orders but does not personally execute the attack.

after.evidence_quote: The Greatjon marches five surviving Karstark prisoners into the hall; three wounded.
after.rationale: The Greatjon physically marches the five surviving Karstark prisoners into the hall.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Ryman's horse shies
after.rationale: Ser Ryman is directly targeted; his horse shies as an immediate consequence of Grey Wind's assault.

after.evidence_quote: Petyr Pimple is thrown
after.rationale: Petyr Pimple is thrown from his horse as a direct result of Grey Wind's lunge.

after.evidence_quote: The Greatjon throws Roslin over his shoulder.
after.rationale: The Greatjon is the named executor who physically carries Roslin from the hall.

after.evidence_quote: Rising to his feet with his leg of lamb, a quarrel goes in his open mouth and out the back of his neck. He crashes forward, sending cups, flagons, and food flying.
after.rationale: Ser Wendel Manderly is explicitly described as being struck by a crossbow quarrel through his open mouth and out the back of his neck, killing him.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Ryman kills Dacey Mormont — He buries his axe head in her stomach.
after.rationale: Ser Ryman Frey directly killed Dacey Mormont by burying his axe head in her stomach.

after.evidence_quote: Daenerys commands Ser Jorah — He is first of her Queensguard, commander of her army
after.rationale: As commander of her army and first of her Queensguard, Jorah is present at the planning assembly and responsible for executing the overall military plan.

after.evidence_quote: Her blue eyes are full of hurt. Jaime thinks she should be grateful.
after.rationale: Brienne is the direct recipient of the arrest, confined to a tower cell against her will, visibly hurt by the action.

after.evidence_quote: Outlaws defer to her for judgment; called 'm'lady'; Lady Stoneheart condemns Merrett Frey — nods when asked if he was part of the Red Wedding.
after.rationale: Lady Stoneheart commands the Brotherhood and is the ultimate judge to whom Merrett's defense is directed; the outlaws defer entirely to her for judgment and she ultimately condemns him.

after.evidence_quote: Dick stands on a crenel for a better shot and is hit by an arrow from below; falls a hundred feet to his death.
after.rationale: Dick is explicitly described as being struck by an arrow and falling from the battlements to his death.

after.evidence_quote: Pyp spots them; Grenn and Pyp roll burning oil barrels over edge
after.rationale: Pyp first spots the attackers and then actively participates in rolling burning barrels over the edge.

after.evidence_quote: Grenn and Pyp roll burning oil barrels over edge
after.rationale: Pyp physically rolls burning oil barrels over the edge alongside Grenn during the mammoth attack.

after.evidence_quote: Chett, Lark, and Small Paul discuss their plan to murder Mormont, Blane, Grubbs, Aethan, Dywen, Bannen, and Samwell Tarly
after.rationale: Lark is a subordinate conspirator physically present in and participating in the plan review discussion.

after.evidence_quote: One bolt punches through the left crown on Dontos's surcoat (chest), another hits his throat, a third his belly.
after.rationale: Dontos is struck by three crossbow bolts — chest, throat, and belly — and his corpse is set ablaze on the boat.

after.evidence_quote: Brune dips his torch … Brune tosses the torch onto the corpse and the boat blazes.
after.rationale: Brune acts as the on-site executor: he receives Littlefinger's order, signals the crossbowmen by dipping his torch, then disposes of the evidence by tossing the torch onto Dontos's corpse.

after.evidence_quote: He thinks Tallad seems decent, and tall; Tallad gazed wistfully at Shae more than once
after.rationale: Ser Tallad is the intended recipient of the arranged marriage and thus a direct target of Tyrion's decision, though the role is a loose fit since the arrangement would benefit rather than harm him.

after.evidence_quote: She tells Ser Boros to let them 'sleep forever.'
after.rationale: Ser Boros is explicitly commanded by Cersei to execute the guards, making him the direct physical agent of the killing.

after.evidence_quote: Septa Scolera strips her bare, even her smallclothes.
after.rationale: Septa Scolera personally performs the stripping of Cersei's clothing.

after.evidence_quote: The High Septon | Imprisons | Cersei | Arrests her on charges of murder, treason, and fornication
after.rationale: The High Septon ordered Cersei's arrest and imprisonment, making him the instigator who did not personally execute the stripping.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Tallad wounds three gold cloaks before being taken.
after.rationale: Ser Tallad resisted and wounded three gold cloaks before being taken into custody.

after.evidence_quote: She writes in ten names: Ser Tallad...
after.rationale: His name is written on an arrest warrant, marking him as one of the accused.

after.evidence_quote: Lord Wyman orders arrest — Guards surround Davos with silver tridents.
after.rationale: The event title explicitly names Lord Wyman as the one who orders the arrest; the guards act on his command.

after.evidence_quote: Iron Emmett | serves | Jon Snow | Physical enforcement; drags Slynt
after.rationale: Iron Emmett provides physical enforcement by dragging Slynt to the block, making him a co-participant in executing the sentence.

after.evidence_quote: Bound with rope and noose, dragged by Ser Godry Farring's horse to the fire pit.
after.rationale: Ser Godry Farring physically performs the action of dragging Mance to the fire pit via his horse.

after.evidence_quote: Tormund surrenders his son Dryn as the hundredth hostage.
after.rationale: Tormund physically surrenders Dryn as the hundredth hostage, performing the act of giving.

after.evidence_quote: Tormund surrenders his son Dryn as the hundredth hostage. Jon promises Dryn will serve as his personal page.
after.rationale: Tormund physically delivers the wildling boys and personally surrenders his own son Dryn as the hundredth hostage, executing the handover from the wildling side.

after.evidence_quote: Tormund points out sons of Soren Shieldbreaker, Gerrick Kingsblood, Harle the Huntsman, Harle the Handsome, Howd Wanderer, Brogg, Devyn Sealskinner, Kyleg of the Wooden Ear, Morna White Mask, the Great Walrus, Alfyn Crowkiller, and Varamyr Sixskins.
after.rationale: Tormund is explicitly the one who points out which hostage boys belong to which notable fathers.

after.evidence_quote: Tormund points out sons of...the Great Walrus...
after.rationale: His son is among the hostages and he is identified by Tormund as a notable father subject to the blood-price arrangement.

after.evidence_quote: Tormund points out sons of...Varamyr Sixskins.
after.rationale: His son is among the hostages and he is identified by Tormund as a notable father subject to the blood-price arrangement.

after.evidence_quote: Ramsay Bolton betrays surrendered ironborn: promises safe conduct then kills all 63 prisoners and stakes their corpses
after.rationale: Ramsay personally promised safe conduct to the surrendered ironborn and then ordered their slaughter — the betrayal is explicitly attributed to him.

after.evidence_quote: The third surrenders but Caggo beheads him with his Valyrian steel arakh, complaining about the noise.
after.rationale: Caggo acts as a direct executor, beheading a surrendered guard with his Valyrian steel arakh.

after.evidence_quote: A second locust is hit by a crossbow bolt.
after.rationale: An unnamed Windblown crossbowman shoots and kills one of the locust guards as part of the attack, acting as a group executor under Caggo.

after.evidence_quote: The third surrenders but Caggo beheads him with his Valyrian steel arakh, complaining about the noise.
after.rationale: Caggo personally beheaded the surrendering third guard with his Valyrian steel arakh.

after.evidence_quote: A second locust is hit by a crossbow bolt.
after.rationale: An unidentified crossbowman (most likely one of the Windblown sellswords present under Caggo's command) shot the second locust with a bolt; the shooter is not named in the text.

after.evidence_quote: he orders the maester's throat cut and body thrown in the sea
after.rationale: Kerwin is the subject of the killing order — his throat is to be cut and his body thrown overboard.

after.evidence_chapter: ADWD The Prince of Winterfell I
after.evidence_quote: Ramsay claims 'Arya' and Theon gives her away… Ramsay removes the Stark cloak and fastens a Bolton cloak.
after.rationale: Ramsay physically performs the wedding rite — he claims 'Arya', removes the Stark cloak, and fastens the Bolton cloak upon her.

after.evidence_quote: Ser Corliss Penny says the gods of the north sent the storm.
after.rationale: He opens the religious argument by attributing the storm to the northern gods, framing the case for why R'hllor must be appeased.

after.evidence_quote: Tyrion dreams of fighting alongside Barristan the Bold and Bittersteel with dragons overhead
after.rationale: Bittersteel fights alongside Tyrion in the dream as a co-combatant.

after.evidence_quote: Holly | loyal to/follows | Abel — Part of his team; executes plan
after.rationale: Abel organized and led the escape mission; Holly acts as part of his team executing his plan, making him the instigating commander even though he did not issue a specific kill order.

after.evidence_quote: kicks him down to save him from stone man
after.rationale: Young Griff is specifically endangered by a stone man during the attack, requiring Tyrion to physically knock him out of harm's way.
